title: El Ibex toma aire con Irán y la Fed de fondo
summary: La tensión con la que comenzó la semana amaina. Los mercados vuelven a confiar en las negociaciones entre EEUU e Irán, sin perder de vista la comparecencia del próximo presidente de la Fed. La tregua del petróleo se extiende a la Bolsa, y al Ibex. Leer

title: El desencuentro en las negociaciones entre EEUU e Irán dispara el petróleo
summary: El presidente de Estados Unidos, Donald Trump, ve "altamente improbable" seguir negociando si no se llega a un acuerdo este miércoles, lo que implicaría el bombardeo de centrales eléctricas y puentes en Irán. Leer

title: Today in Spain: A roundup of the latest news on Tuesday
summary: Police officer dies in Guadalajara, seismologist claims Rosalía concerts caused Barcelona tremors, and more news from Spain on Tuesday April 21st.

title: LATEST: Spanish airports operate amid air traffic controllers strike
summary: Several Spanish airports were hit by industrial action by air traffic controllers on Monday, but initial reports suggested there had been little to no impact on fights.

title: 86 percent applying for Spain's migrant amnesty are Latino, data shows
summary: Estimates from migration think tanks show that the vast majority of those set to benefit from Spain's migrant regularisation are Latin American, data that somewhat debunks misinformation about the measure.

title: Gibraltar prepares high-tech border ahead of Brexit deal rollout
summary: The new technology, which includes facial recognition cameras and security lighting, was announced just months before the implementation of long-awaited post-Brexit border arrangements on The Rock.

title: What to do in Spain if your flight is delayed due to strikes
summary: More airport walkouts have started in Spain, affecting 14 of the country's smaller airports. Here's what you need to know if your flight is delayed.

title: Spain urges EU to end association agreement with Israel
summary: Spain will ask the European Union to end its association agreement with Israel over alleged violations of international law, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said on Sunday.

title: Pan: Seven everyday Spanish expressions with the word 'bread'
summary: What does 'eaten bread' mean in Spain? Or to 'make good crumbs'? As bread is such a staple of the Spanish diet, it's no surprise there are loads of expressions with the word 'pan', but most have nothing to do with the food.

title: Leftist leaders gather in Spain to rally against rise of far right
summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez and Brazil's President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva will head a meeting of leftist leaders on Saturday in Barcelona, seeking to rally against the threat to democracy from the far right.
